key_insight: The paper replaces slow per-instance stochastic optimization with a trained neural proxy that predicts near-optimal decisions in one forward pass while preserving feasibility through decoding.
engineering_relevance: This enables sub-second order-fulfillment decisions under uncertainty, making real-time omnichannel logistics planning practical at scale.
method: Build a scenario-embedded neural network trained offline on solver-generated labels from contextual sample average approximation (C-SAA).
Use an online decoder to enforce feasibility before executing the predicted fulfillment action.
Train with a composite loss combining label imitation, constraint-violation penalties, and self-supervised cost alignment.
evidence_signal: In a calibrated simulator based on JD.com transactional records, the proxy was about 2800x faster than online finite-sample C-SAA, improved realized fulfillment cost by 3.3% over the reference, and cut late deliveries roughly in half versus established policies.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of solver-generated labels and simulator calibration, so deployment may require retraining or revalidation when demand patterns, carrier behavior, or network constraints shift.
paper_title: Learning Optimization Proxies for Sequential Contextual Stochastic Programs: An Order Fulfillment Application
